sci_1.title: Space Technology - ISRO Launch Vehicles, Satellites & Orbits

subtopics.name: PSLV vs GSLV Fuel Stratification
bullets: Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV): ISRO's highly reliable 4-stage workhorse. It features an alternating fuel profile: the 1st and 3rd stages use solid Hydroxyl-terminated polybutadiene (HTPB) propellant, while the 2nd and 4th stages rely on liquid **UH25 fuel mixed with Dinitrogen Tetroxide ($N_2O_4$)** oxidizers in indigenous Vikas engines.

Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV): A heavy-lift 3-stage configuration designed for higher Geosynchronous Transfer Orbits (GTO). It features solid boosters, a liquid second stage, and a highly complex **Cryogenic Upper Stage (CUS)** that ignites sub-zero liquid hydrogen ($LH_2$) fuel and liquid oxygen ($LOX$) oxidizers for maximum thrust efficiency.

LVM3 (Launch Vehicle Mark 3): India's heaviest operational lifter. It bypasses lower-capacity tracking by utilizing twin massive S200 solid strap-on boosters, an L110 liquid core, and a C25 cryogenic upper stage, capable of deploying 4-ton payloads directly into GTO.

subtopics.name: Orbital Altitude Classifications
bullets: Low Earth Orbit (LEO): Altitudes ranging from 160 km to 2,000 km. Host to remote sensing, earth observation satellites, and the International Space Station (ISS) due to low propagation delay.

Geosynchronous (GSO) vs Geostationary (GEO): Both orbit at exactly **35,786 km** with an orbital period matching Earth's rotation (23 hours, 56 minutes, 4 seconds). **UPSC Trap:** A GEO satellite must be placed directly over the **equator with a 0° inclination**, appearing completely stationary to a ground antenna. A GSO satellite has an inclined orbit and traces a figure-8 path relative to the ground.

subtopics.name: Integrated Guided Missile Program & MIRV Architecture
bullets: IGMDP Blueprint: Conceived by Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam, developing 5 core systems: 1. Prithvi: Surface-to-surface short-range ballistic missile. 2. Agni: Surface-to-surface intermediate-to-intercontinental system. 3. Trishul: Short-range surface-to-air. 4. Akash: Medium-range surface-to-air using ramjet propulsion. 5. Nag: Third-generation anti-tank 'fire-and-forget' missile.

Cruise vs Ballistic Trajectories: Cruise missiles (e.g., BrahMos) fly at low altitudes within the atmosphere along flat paths, powered continuously by jet engines. Ballistic missiles (e.g., Agni) follow a high parabolic trajectory, leaving the atmosphere before gravity pulls the unpowered payload down onto the target.

MIRV Technology (Agni-V): India mastered **Multiple Independently Targetable Re-entry Vehicles (MIRVs)** under Mission Divyastra. It allows a single ballistic missile to carry multiple nuclear warheads, each capable of striking completely separate targets hundreds of kilometers apart.

Nuclear Triad Submarines: Completed via the deployment of INS Arihant, a nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine (SSBN). Unlike diesel-electric submarines (SSKs) that must surface frequently to recharge batteries, nuclear submarines can remain submerged indefinitely, providing a survivable second-strike capability.

sci_3.title: Nuclear Technology - Nuclear Fission, Fusion & India's 3-Stage Program

phases.phaseName: Reactor Physics & Domestic Strategic Fuel Cycles

subtopics.name: Homi Bhabha's Three-Stage Nuclear Strategy
bullets: Stage 1 - PHWRs: Utilizes **Pressurized Heavy Water Reactors** fueled by un-enriched, **Natural Uranium ($U^{238}$)**. It produces electricity while transmuting $U^{238}$ into a byproduct: **Plutonium-239 ($Pu^{239}$)**. Heavy water ($D_2O$) acts as both the coolant and neutron moderator.

Stage 2 - FBRs: Relies on **Fast Breeder Reactors** fueled by a Mixed Oxide (MOX) combination of the $Pu^{239}$ recovered from Stage 1 and a surrounding blanket of local **Thorium-232 ($Th^{232}$)**. It is called a 'breeder' because it creates more fissile Uranium-233 ($U^{233}$) than it consumes.

Stage 3 - Thorium Reactors: The ultimate long-term stage, utilizing reactors fueled by **Uranium-233 bred in Stage 2, surrounded by a Thorium blanket** to convert fresh Thorium into fissile fuel, creating a self-sustaining cycle. This leverages India's massive domestic Monazite sand Thorium reserves.

Fission vs Fusion Traps: Nuclear fission splits heavy, unstable atomic nuclei (like $U^{235}$) into lighter elements, leaving radioactive waste. Nuclear fusion forces light isotopes (like Deuterium and Tritium) together under extreme temperature and pressure to form Helium, generating clean energy without long-lived high-level waste (tracked globally via the **ITER project**).

subtopics.name: CRISPR-Cas9 Mechanics & Somatic vs Germline Editing
bullets: CRISPR-Cas9 Gene Drive: A molecular gene-editing technology. It uses a **Guide RNA (gRNA)** sequence to find a specific target location on a DNA strand, where the **Cas9 endonuclease protein** acts as genetic scissors to snip the double helix, allowing precise insertion, deletion, or repair of genes.

Somatic vs Germline Modifications: Somatic Editing targets non-reproductive cells (like blood or skin tissues) to cure disease in a single patient; these edits **cannot be passed down** to future generations. Germline Editing targets reproductive cells (sperm, eggs, or early embryos); these genetic modifications **alter the hereditary line permanently**.

Recombinant DNA Tech: Involves joining DNA molecules from two different species and inserting the hybrid string into a host organism (e.g., using plasmids in E. coli bacteria to mass-produce human insulin).

sci_5.title: Information Technology, Quantum Computing & Emerging Tech (AI, Blockchain)

subtopics.name: Quantum Superposition & Decentralized Ledgers
bullets: Quantum Qubit Physics: Classical computers rely on silicon transistors processing binary bits as either a 0 or a 1. Quantum computing utilizes subatomic qubits that leverage **Superposition** (existing simultaneously as both 0 and 1) and **Entanglement** (qubits remaining linked regardless of distance), expanding computing power exponentially.

Blockchain Cryptography: A decentralized, distributed ledger system. Data is stored in chronological blocks secured using cryptographic hashes. Every block is linked immutably to the preceding one, creating a system that cannot be altered retroactively without rewriting the entire network history.

Web 3.0 vs Web 2.0: Web 2.0 is the centralized, interactive web dominated by tech platforms hosting user data. Web 3.0 is a decentralized internet architecture built on blockchain nodes, where users maintain sovereign ownership over their own data and identities through smart contracts.

subtopics.name: Quantum Confinement Effects & Graphene Anomalies
bullets: The Nanoscale Shift: At the nanoscale (1 to 100 nanometers), materials stop following classical physics and begin exhibiting **Quantum Confinement** effects. This changes their optical properties, electrical conductivity, and chemical reactivity relative to their bulk states.

Graphene Allotropy: A two-dimensional, single-atom-thick sheet of carbon arranged in a hexagonal honeycomb lattice. It is a phenomenal conductor of heat and electricity, flexible, and holds a tensile strength **200 times greater than steel**.

Superconductivity Thresholds: A physical state where certain materials exhibit **zero electrical resistance** and expel all internal magnetic fields when cooled below a specific Critical Temperature ($T_c$)—an effect known as the **Meissner Effect**.

sci_7.title: Health & Disease - Viruses, Bacteria, Vaccines & Antimicrobial Resistance

subtopics.name: Antimicrobial Resistance & Vaccine Engineering
bullets: Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR): Occurs when bacteria, viruses, or parasites mutate over time, rendering standard antibiotic medications completely ineffective. Driven by the misuse of antibiotics and **horizontal gene transfer** through plasmids (such as the spread of the NDM-1 superbug gene).

Vaccine Platform Configurations: 1. Inactivated Vaccines: Uses pathogens killed by heat or chemicals (e.g., Covaxin). 2. Viral Vector Vaccines: Uses a safe virus (like an adenovirus) as a delivery vehicle to carry genetic instructions for a pathogen's spike protein (e.g., Covishield). 3. mRNA Vaccines: Delivers synthetic messenger RNA strands wrapped in lipid nanoparticles to instruct cells to produce defensive antigens.

Zoonotic Spillovers: Infectious diseases caused by pathogens that jump directly from animal hosts to humans, often driven by deforestation, habitat fragmentation, and wildlife markets (e.g., Nipah, Ebola, and Avian Influenza).
